summary: Auditing deferred destruction and readback cleanup invariants.
Auditing execution-v0 session default contract enforcement and runtime merge semantics.
compileExecutionV0 backfills missing manifest sessionDefaults fields from schema/runtime defaults, silently rewriting execution-v0 contract meaning for compute defaults and decodeLoop fields.
Auditing CLI bench parity defaults
Auditing browser relay parity and harness fallbacks
Auditing compare-engine shared contract and fallback behavior
parity: CLI injects default bench modelId and browser surface, bypassing shared required-request and auto-surface contract
artifact-schema: CLI JSON error envelope reports surface=null for auto runs, masking the actual failing surface
harness: browser relay silently downgrades OPFS or persistent-cache failures into HTTP/non-persistent runs by mutating request.loadMode
artifact-schema: browser relay post-processes result env/environment metadata, so success payload shape depends on transport
methodology: compare-engines merges arbitrary runtime-config-json after the shared contract, allowing Doppler-only fairness drift
Auditing manifest decodeLoop enforcement, kernel-path dtype validation, and runtime rewrite behavior.
Auditing converter defaultKernelPath validation and preset-driven dtype compatibility checks.
Auditing manifest inference kernel-path selection for implicit compute-dtype defaults.
Manifest decodeLoop silently drops invalid or null ring buffer fields instead of failing or preserving explicit null disables, which rewrites session batching policy in JS.
Kernel-path dtype validation hardcodes an LFM2 mixed-precision exception in JS, allowing manifest/kernel incompatibility to continue instead of failing through config or rule assets.
Converter defaultKernelPath validation hardcodes an LFM2 f32->f16 exception, allowing incompatible manifest defaults to be emitted without an explicit asset-level rule.

resolveKernelPathFromPreset defaults missing quantizationInfo.compute to f16 for compute-keyed kernel-path maps, inventing converter policy in JS instead of requiring explicit config or preset defaults.
methodology: compare-engines silently retries Doppler with no OPFS cache after invalid-manifest failures, changing load semantics mid-benchmark
CLI audit complete; parity and error-envelope issues logged
Browser relay audit complete; harness fallback and payload-shape issues logged
Compare-engine audit complete; methodology issues logged
Deferred destruction cleared pending buffers without destroying them on queue failure; readback staging could remain active on map failure.
Centralized tracked-buffer release and pending-destruction cleanup so queue/map failures still destroy staging and deferred buffers.
Verified with tests/inference/buffer-pool-cleanup.test.js.
Auditing vendor harness metric mappings
Auditing vendor release-matrix freshness and contract tracking
methodology: Doppler and TJS harnesses map prefillTokensPerSec from different phase semantics, so the required prefill comparison is apples-to-oranges
artifact-schema: release-matrix freshness and compare-artifact ingestion ignore harness-contract changes, so stale evidence can look current
Vendor harness mapping audit complete; methodology issue logged
Vendor release-matrix audit complete; freshness issue logged
Runtime cleanup audit complete; deferred-destruction and readback failure paths verified.

Added explicit execution-v0 manifest sessionDefaults validation so missing compute defaults, kvcache.kvDtype, and incomplete decodeLoop fields fail before merge instead of inheriting schema/runtime defaults.
Auditing kernel-path resolution for hidden dtype policy rewrites.

resolveKernelPathState rewrites runtime activationDtype, kvcache.kvDtype, and session outputDtype from kernelPath metadata after selection, so kernel policy is still being invented in JS instead of resolved as explicit config.
Targeted execution-v0 tests and execution-contract checks passed after manifest sessionDefaults validation was added.
Execution-v0 contract audit complete for this pass; silent session-default backfill removed and covered by regression tests.
Model-load audit pass complete; kernel-path dtype rewrite remains open because it needs explicit source tracking or contract relocation instead of another silent bridge.
Auditing BF16 conversion helper and dtype rule resolution.
Loader BF16 conversion helper still contained ad hoc GPU readback tracing, and dtype rule lookup depended on input casing.
Removed throwaway BF16 probe code and normalized rule lookup casing before applying runtime dtype tags.
Verified with tests/loader/dtype-utils.test.js.
Loader dtype helper audit complete; BF16 helper and runtime dtype tagging invariants verified.
Rechecked decodeLoop ring fields: null disable values are preserved explicitly and invalid values fail; added regression coverage for null ring disables and invalid ringTokens.
Rechecked manifest kernel-path dtype validation: the LFM2 mixed-precision JS exception is gone and mismatch now fails fast; regression coverage asserts the incompatibility error.

Config-selected kernel paths now fail unless matching activation/kv/output dtypes are explicit; manifest/model kernel paths may seed only default runtime dtypes, and runtime kernel presets now declare matching dtype config.
Targeted model-load and preset contract tests passed after kernel-path dtype contract tightening and preset alignment.
Model-load contract audit complete for this pass; decodeLoop semantics, manifest dtype validation, and kernel-path runtime dtype handling are now explicit and covered by regression tests.
Removed CLI bench modelId/browser defaults and restored fail-fast surface resolution.
Error envelopes now promote the actual failing surface from normalized error details.
Verified with tests/integration/cli-surface-contract.test.js and tests/integration/command-envelope.test.js.
Browser relay now fails closed on persistent-OPFS/cache-priming failures instead of silently switching to HTTP/non-persistent mode.
Removed relay-only result env/environment mutation so success payloads stay transport-stable.
Verified with tests/integration/node-browser-command-relay-contract.test.js and tests/integration/browser-convert-command-surface.test.js.
Blocked runtime-config-json overrides from mutating compare-managed fairness and declared Doppler cadence fields.
Removed silent no-opfs retry and made decode stop-check policy explicit in benchmark policy/reporting.
Aligned compare prefill mapping to TTFT-normalized timing on both Doppler and TJS harnesses.
Verified with tests/config/compare-engines-cli-contract.test.js.
Release-matrix freshness now includes harness and compare-contract sources, and compare-artifact ingestion rejects stale contract metadata.
Verified with tests/config/vendor-bench-cli-contract.test.js and tools/vendor-bench.js validate.
Verified with tests/config/compare-engines-cli-contract.test.js and tools/vendor-bench.js validate.

methodology/artifact-schema: compare artifacts now claim promptTokensPerSecToFirstToken through the shared compare-metrics contract, retain raw prefill timing only as engine-local payload data, and refresh committed compare fixtures against the pinned metric/harness hashes. Verified with tests/config/compare-engines-cli-contract.test.js.

artifact-schema: release-matrix generation and compare-artifact validation now track compare-metric plus harness hashes and reject stale committed compare fixtures. Verified with tests/config/vendor-bench-cli-contract.test.js and tools/vendor-bench.js validate.

methodology/artifact-schema: shared compare metric contract now separates apples-to-apples prompt-throughput claims from engine-local raw prefill timing, with fixtures and benchmark docs refreshed to match.
Auditing recorder cleanup under queue failure and profiling readback teardown.
Adding explicit discard path for active pooled buffers during recorder failure cleanup.
Shared runtime lacked an explicit discard path for active pooled buffers on error cleanup, forcing failure handlers to choose between leaking tracking state or returning invalid buffers to the pool.
Added BufferPool.discard() and discardBuffer() so recorder/device-loss cleanup can force-destroy active pooled buffers without corrupting pool tracking.
Verified with tests/inference/buffer-pool-cleanup.test.js and tests/gpu/command-recorder-cleanup.test.js.
Error-path buffer-pool audit complete; active-buffer discard contract verified.

Recorder deferred cleanup only logged queue failures, leaving temp buffers, pooled buffers, and pending uniform-cache evictions unreleased; profiling teardown also leaked resources when readback was skipped or failed.
Centralized recorder cleanup so queue failures still dispose tracked buffers, and moved profiling readback teardown into finally paths so query/readback resources are always destroyed.
Verified with tests/gpu/command-recorder-cleanup.test.js.
Recorder cleanup audit complete; deferred cleanup and profiling teardown invariants verified.
Auditing delivery-plan config normalization for hidden source-order and retry-policy fallback.

harness: distribution delivery silently dropped unsupported sourceOrder entries and rewrote invalid retry/abuse/source-decision config to defaults, changing transport policy without surfacing an error.
Delivery planning now fails closed on invalid sourceOrder entries, invalid decision-trace sampling rates, and invalid HTTP/P2P retry-policy integers instead of silently rewriting them.
Verified with tests/integration/distribution-shard-delivery.test.js.
Distribution delivery config audit complete for this pass; source-order and retry-policy rewrites are now fail-closed and covered by regression tests.
Auditing control-plane config normalization for hidden token refresh fallback.
harness: normalizeP2PControlPlaneConfig silently rewrote invalid tokenRefreshSkewMs values to the default skew, changing control-plane refresh cadence without surfacing config errors.
Control-plane config now rejects invalid tokenRefreshSkewMs values instead of defaulting them silently.
Verified with tests/integration/p2p-control-plane.test.js.
Control-plane config audit complete for this pass; refresh-skew normalization is now fail-closed and covered by regression tests.
Auditing hot-swap rollout policy normalization for hidden mode and canary-percent rewrites.
harness: hot-swap rollout policy silently rewrote invalid rollout.mode to shadow and clamped out-of-range canaryPercent, changing rollout behavior without surfacing a contract error.
Hot-swap rollout evaluation now rejects invalid rollout.mode and out-of-range canaryPercent instead of rewriting them silently.
Verified with tests/integration/hotswap-rollout.test.js.
Hot-swap rollout audit complete for this pass; rollout-policy rewrites are now fail-closed and covered by regression tests.
Auditing published tooling subpath exports for browser-vs-Node contract drift.

parity: package export ./tooling routed browser consumers to the Node entry, and the browser import-graph gate did not cover the browser tooling subpath, so browser bundles could pull Node-only tooling modules silently.
Added a browser-conditioned tooling export and browser-subpath import-graph/test coverage so browser imports resolve the shared browser-safe tooling entry.
Verified with tests/integration/tooling-browser-export-surface.test.js and npm run imports:check:browser.
Tooling export-map audit complete for this pass; browser-conditioned tooling imports now fail closed against Node-only helpers and are covered by regression tests.
Auditing compare-contract secondary surfaces for stale prompt-throughput and harness-path assumptions.

methodology/artifact-schema: compare-contract rename to promptTokensPerSecToFirstToken was incomplete across onboarding validation, compare-chart fallbacks, capability metadata, and generated release-matrix artifacts; onboarding still rejected ordered fallback harness paths.

Aligned onboarding validation, compare-chart fallbacks, capability metadata, and regenerated release-matrix artifacts with the promptTokensPerSecToFirstToken compare contract and ordered harness fallback paths.
Verified with tests/config/onboarding-tooling-contract.test.js, tests/config/vendor-bench-cli-contract.test.js, tools/vendor-bench.js matrix, and tools/vendor-bench.js validate.
Compare-contract secondary-surface audit complete for this pass; onboarding, chart fallback, capability metadata, and generated release docs now match the shared compare metric contract.
Capability-matrix compare-metric audit complete for this pass; prompt-throughput compare support is now represented explicitly and regenerated into release-matrix artifacts.
Auditing shared runtime-input application for calibrate-intent instrumentation drift across CLI, browser, Node, and demo callers.
harness: calibrate runs depended on caller/runtime-validator order for investigation-instrumentation rejection, and demo diagnostics masked invalid bench configs by rewriting them silently.
Shared runtime input application now rejects calibrate-intent instrumentation after preset/url/runtime merges so all surfaces fail closed consistently.
Demo diagnostics no longer sanitize bench runtime configs and now mirror the shared modelId requirement instead of allowing modelUrl-only bench/debug requests.
Verified with tests/integration/command-runner-shared.test.js and tests/integration/diagnostics-controller-contract.test.js.
parity: tooling API docs described debug/bench as accepting modelUrl-only requests even though the shared command contract requires modelId and treats modelUrl as optional source metadata.
Tooling API docs now describe modelId as the required harnessed-command key and modelUrl as an optional explicit source.
harness: getting-started instructed browser relay for local file:// converted-artifact verification even though local filesystem verification is a Node-surface workflow.
Getting-started now routes local converted-artifact verification through the Node surface and documents the file:// fetch shim dependency explicitly.
Verified against src/tooling/node-file-fetch.js and tests/integration/shader-cache-node-file-url.test.js.
Auditing KV cache GPU->CPU sync readback cleanup and dtype handling.
Auditing profiler readback teardown and pending-resolve cleanup.
Auditing large-gradient accumulation cleanup under early GPU failure.
Auditing external tensor-map normalization and parser/runtime compatibility.
Auditing span assembly invariants and single-shard fallback behavior.
Auditing tensors.json loading path for normalized shard fields.

KV cache GPU->CPU sync duplicated manual staging-buffer readback and cleanup instead of using the shared runtime readback helper, leaving inference-side cleanup behavior to drift from memory/buffer-pool.
Centralized KV-cache sync readback onto memory/buffer-pool.readBuffer() and added a regression covering the non-paged GPU sync path.
Verified with tests/inference/kv-cache-sync.test.js.
KV cache sync audit complete; shared readback contract verified.
Auditing embed debug readback path for shared helper reuse and cleanup invariants.
Auditing probe tensor readback path for manual staging cleanup drift.
Probe tensor inspection duplicated manual aligned staging-buffer readback instead of using the shared readback helper, risking cleanup drift in debug-only runtime paths.
Moved probe readback onto memory/buffer-pool.readBufferSlice() and added a regression covering GPU-backed probe logging.
Verified with tests/inference/probes-readback.test.js.
Probe readback audit complete; shared readback contract verified.
Embed debug sampling path manually staged GPU readback instead of reusing the shared runtime readback helper.
Moved embed debug sampling onto memory/buffer-pool.readBuffer() to keep debug readback cleanup consistent with shared runtime helpers.
Verified indirectly through shared readback regressions covering buffer-pool and probe paths.
Embed debug readback audit complete; helper reuse aligned with shared runtime cleanup.
Auditing layer debug readback path for manual staging cleanup drift.
Auditing prefill debug readback paths for shared helper reuse.
Layer attention-output debug sampling duplicated manual staging-buffer readback instead of using the shared runtime helper.
Moved layer debug readback onto memory/buffer-pool.readBuffer() to keep debug cleanup aligned with the shared runtime path.
Verified through shared readback regressions covering buffer-pool and probe integration.
Layer debug readback audit complete; helper reuse aligned with shared cleanup.
Generator prefill debug sampling paths duplicated manual staging-buffer readback instead of using shared runtime helpers.
Moved prefill debug hidden-state sampling onto memory/buffer-pool.readBufferSlice() to keep debug cleanup and alignment handling centralized.
Verified through shared readback regressions covering buffer-pool, probes, and KV-cache integration.
Generator debug readback audit complete; helper reuse aligned with shared cleanup.
External tensor-map parsing accepted legacy shard aliases at the top level but did not normalize legacy span shard fields, leaving runtime loaders exposed to alias drift.
Normalized legacy top-level and span shard aliases while validating tensors.json tensor locations before runtime consumption.
Verified with tests/loader/rdrr-tensor-map-reader.test.js.
Auditing kernel test readback helper for cleanup drift from runtime helper semantics.
RDRR tensor-map parsing audit complete; shard alias normalization and runtime-facing validation invariants verified.
Inline manifest tensor locations still passed raw span metadata through without normalizing legacy span.shard aliases, so runtime prefetch and streaming paths could read undefined shard indices.
Centralized tensor-location span normalization in buildTensorLocations() so tensors.json and inline manifest.tensors produce the same runtime shard metadata.
Verified with tests/loader/rdrr-tensor-map-reader.test.js covering tensors.json and inline manifest tensor locations.
Loader shard-resolver audit complete; tensor-location normalization is now consistent across external and inline metadata sources.
Tensor assembly treated empty spans as a multi-span tensor and assumed top-level shardIndex was already normalized, leaving malformed or legacy tensor locations to fail inconsistently at runtime.
Validated single-shard location fields explicitly, accepted the legacy top-level shard alias, treated empty spans as single-shard fallback, and enforced span-total byte invariants.
Kernel test readback helper destroyed staging buffers only on the success path, leaking them on map/read failures.
Verified with tests/loader/rdrr-tensor-map-reader.test.js covering empty spans, legacy shard aliases, and mismatched span sizes.
Wrapped kernel test staging readback in finally cleanup and added a regression for map failure.
Tensor reader audit complete; shard assembly now fails fast on malformed metadata and preserves single-shard runtime behavior for legacy aliases.
Verified with tests/kernels/buffer-utils-readback.test.js.
Kernel test buffer readback helper audit complete; failure cleanup verified.
Profiler timestamp resolve left pending query state uncleared on readback skip or map failure, risking stale indices across later resolve calls.
Wrapped profiler resolve readback in finally cleanup so mapped buffers unmap conditionally and pending query state resets even when readback is skipped or mapAsync rejects.
Verified with node tests/gpu/profiler-cleanup.test.js.
Verified with tests/gpu/profiler-cleanup.test.js.
Profiler cleanup audit complete; resolve-state reset and readback teardown invariants verified.
Profiler readback cleanup audit complete; pending query state now resets deterministically across skip and failure paths.
Auditing single-token stop-check kernel for staging readback cleanup and ownership invariants.
checkStop() destroyed uniform and output buffers only on the success path, leaking staging resources when readback mapping failed.
Moved stop-check readback and owned-buffer teardown under try/finally so temporary buffers are destroyed on mapAsync failure and caller-owned buffers remain preserved.
Verified with node tests/gpu/check-stop-cleanup.test.js.
Stop-check kernel wrapper audit complete; readback failure cleanup verified.
Auditing GPU sample/argmax runtime path for centralized readback cleanup and pooled buffer teardown on failure.
GPU argmax/sample duplicated staging readback and success-only teardown, leaving pooled temporaries active when mapAsync failed.
Centralized sample-run readback/teardown in shared helpers so staging buffers, uniforms, and owned pooled outputs are always released after argmax and GPU sampling runs.
Verified with node tests/gpu/sample-cleanup.test.js.
GPU sampling wrapper audit complete; centralized readback cleanup verified for argmax and stochastic sampling paths.
Verified profiler resolve() readback teardown and pending-resolve reset with tests/gpu/profiler-cleanup.test.js.
Profiler audit complete; readback teardown and pending-resolve cleanup verified.
Auditing check-stop GPU sampling readback for shared helper reuse and cleanup invariants.
checkStop() duplicated manual staging-buffer readback instead of using the shared runtime readback helper, keeping a second cleanup path alive in GPU sampling.
Moved checkStop() readback onto memory/buffer-pool.readBufferSlice() so staging allocation, map cleanup, and failure handling stay centralized.
Verified with tests/gpu/check-stop-cleanup.test.js after updating the fake queue contract for shared buffer-pool readback.
Check-stop kernel wrapper audit complete; readback helper reuse and cleanup invariants verified.
Auditing GPU sample/argmax readback path for shared helper reuse and cleanup drift.

Reviewed chunked gradient accumulation cleanup paths; temporary chunk buffers and output ownership are released deterministically on submit failure. Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
GPU sample and argmax kept a bespoke staging-buffer readback helper instead of reusing the shared runtime readback path, duplicating cleanup logic.
Auditing CPU attention-backward runtime helpers for upload/readback cleanup invariants.
Moved GPU sample/argmax token readback onto memory/buffer-pool.readBufferSlice() and kept temporary buffer release in one shared cleanup path.
Reviewed CPU attention-backward upload/readback helpers; gradient output buffers release correctly on allocation or upload failure. Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Verified with tests/gpu/sample-cleanup.test.js after refactoring runArgmax() and runGPUSample() onto shared readback helpers.
Auditing optimizer state allocation for pooled-buffer cleanup on partial construction failure.
Sample kernel wrapper audit complete; readback helper reuse and cleanup invariants verified.
Reviewed Adam state allocation path; moment buffers are released on partial initialization failure. Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Auditing LoRA runtime allocation/dispose paths for pooled-buffer ownership invariants.
Reviewed LoRA adapter buffer construction and dispose paths; partial initialization releases pooled buffers correctly. Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Auditing training token-batch tensor upload path for partial-buffer cleanup on write failures.
Reviewed token-batch tensor upload path; input/target buffers are released correctly when uploadData fails mid-construction. Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Auditing diffusion buffer helpers for cleanup on upload failure and temporary-buffer ownership drift.
createDiffusionIndexBuffer() allocated raw GPU buffers but did not destroy them when queue.writeBuffer() failed, leaking runtime temporaries in text-encoder setup paths.
Wrapped diffusion index-buffer upload in try/catch so newly created GPU buffers are destroyed when queue.writeBuffer() fails before pipeline setup completes.
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js and tests/integration/diffusion-runtime-contract.test.js.
Diffusion helper audit complete; upload-failure cleanup now preserves temporary-buffer invariants.
Auditing energy row-head GPU tensor upload helpers for pooled-buffer cleanup on write failures.
createFeatureTensor() acquired pooled buffers and then called queue.writeBuffer() without releasing them on failure, leaking pooled state before GPU scoring began.
Wrapped row-head feature upload in try/catch so pooled buffers are released when queue.writeBuffer() fails during GPU score setup.
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js and tests/inference/dream-energy-head-pipeline.test.js.
Energy row-head pipeline audit complete; GPU upload failures no longer leak pooled feature buffers.
Auditing energy tensor-upload helpers for pooled-buffer cleanup on GPU initialization failures.
createEnergyTensor() acquired pooled buffers and then called queue.writeBuffer() without cleanup, leaking pooled tensors when Quintel GPU initialization failed before fallback.
Wrapped energy tensor upload in try/catch so pooled buffers are released when GPU state initialization fails during queue.writeBuffer().
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js.
Energy pipeline audit complete; GPU init failure cleanup now preserves pooled-buffer invariants before CPU fallback.
Auditing diffusion helper upload buffer creation for writeBuffer failure cleanup.
createDiffusionIndexBuffer() left a newly created GPU buffer alive when queue.writeBuffer() threw, leaking wrapper-owned upload buffers on runtime write failures.
Wrapped diffusion index-buffer upload in try/catch and destroy() so writeBuffer failures do not leak wrapper-owned buffers.
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js.
Diffusion helper audit complete; upload buffer cleanup on write failure verified.
Auditing energy-head feature tensor upload for writeBuffer failure cleanup.
Replaced fail-open active kernel-path policy normalization with shared config validation so legacy source aliases, invalid modes, and non-object policy payloads fail instead of coercing.
Verified strict kernel-path policy contract enforcement with tests/config/kernel-path-loader-contract.test.js and tests/config/merge-contract-check.test.js.
Removed the implicit generic transformer detection fallback. Unknown families now return no preset and resolveConfig fails with an explicit preset-required error.
Verified unknown-family fail-closed detection with tests/config/model-preset-loader.test.js and tests/converter/conversion-plan.test.js.
validateManifestInference now enforces required manifest inference fields for non-diffusion manifests at loader entry instead of checking only inference presence.
Verified loader-entry manifest inference completeness checks with tests/config/manifest-schema-contract.test.js and tests/config/required-inference-fields-contract-check.test.js.

Confirmed compute-keyed kernel-path resolution now rejects missing quantizationInfo.compute and removed the remaining fused->dequant q4k layout rewrite so incompatible preset mappings fail explicitly.
Verified fail-closed converter kernel-path resolution with tests/converter/conversion-plan.test.js.
createFeatureTensor() acquired a pooled buffer and then wrote into it without releasing on queue.writeBuffer() failure, leaking runtime upload buffers on error paths.
Wrapped feature tensor upload in try/catch and releaseBuffer() so padded and unpadded writeBuffer failures clean up pooled buffers consistently.
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js.
Energy-head row pipeline audit complete; feature tensor upload cleanup verified.
Auditing energy tensor upload path for writeBuffer failure cleanup.
createEnergyTensor() acquired a pooled buffer and then wrote into it without releasing on queue.writeBuffer() failure, leaking runtime upload buffers on error paths.
Wrapped energy tensor upload in try/catch and releaseBuffer() so padded and unpadded writeBuffer failures clean up pooled buffers consistently.
Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js.
Energy pipeline audit complete; tensor upload cleanup on write failure verified.
Reviewed generation entrypoint re-exports; no independent runtime logic beyond forwarding to underlying pipeline modules.
Auditing shared training tensor upload helper for centralized buffer-write cleanup.
Reviewed diffusion entrypoint re-exports; no independent buffer or execution logic beyond forwarding to pipeline modules.
Training runtime paths repeatedly duplicated acquireBuffer()+uploadData()+createTensor() with ad hoc release-on-error handling, risking cleanup drift across objectives and adapter pipelines.
Reviewed image-regression metric helpers; pure CPU comparison logic with explicit length/tolerance checks and no runtime resource ownership risks.
Added createUploadedTensor() to centralize training tensor upload allocation, uploadData(), createTensor(), and releaseBuffer() cleanup across runtime call sites.
Reviewed energy entrypoint re-exports; no independent runtime logic beyond forwarding to pipeline modules.
Verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js covering helper-backed training upload failure paths.
Reviewed format entrypoint re-exports; no independent parsing or runtime resource logic in this module.
Training tensor-factory audit complete; shared upload helper verified.
Reviewed RDRR entrypoint re-exports; parsing/validation work is owned by the underlying modules already audited separately.

Reviewed diffusion helper buffer ownership paths; index-buffer creation and recorder/non-recorder release helpers fail cleanly on write errors. Verified with tests/inference/pipeline-buffer-write-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Migrated training tensor uploads onto src/training/tensor-factory.createUploadedTensor() and verified with tests/training/runtime-cleanup.test.js.
Auditing shared command normalization for suite contract rewrites and ignored convert runtime fields.

normalizeToolingCommandRequest() now rejects conflicting explicit suite values for fixed-suite commands instead of silently rewriting them, and convert now rejects runtimePreset/runtimeConfigUrl/runtimeConfig because node convert ignores those inputs.

Verified stricter suite and convert contract enforcement with tests/integration/command-api.test.js, tests/integration/cli-surface-contract.test.js, tests/integration/browser-convert-command-surface.test.js, tests/integration/training-command-malformed-surface.test.js, and tests/integration/tooling-browser-export-surface.test.js.
Shared command API audit complete for suite rewrite and convert runtime contract drift.
Auditing browser convenience API for node/browser contract parity on load-affecting options and model listing.
doppler.text() now rejects load-affecting runtime options like the node API, and browser listModels() now returns canonical modelId strings instead of alias-first results.
Verified browser/node convenience API parity with tests/integration/doppler-browser-api-parity.test.js.
Browser convenience API parity audit complete for load-affecting option rejection and listModels output contract.
Reviewing browser harness manifest/runtime input handling for parity with shared command runner semantics.

runBrowserManifest()/applyRuntimeForRun() apply exactly one of runtimeConfig, runtimeConfigUrl, or runtimePreset and skip configChain, which diverges from shared command runner merge order (preset -> configUrl -> runtimeConfig -> contract patch) and can silently change harnessed manifest behavior.
Browser harness needs follow-up: manifest/runtime input merge order and configChain handling are not aligned with shared command-runner semantics.
Reviewing browser provider load/cache surface behavior for explicit source selection and config opt-in.

When an explicit modelUrl is provided and remote manifest comparison fails, loadModel() logs and reuses the cached artifact instead of failing closed, so explicit source selection can silently drift to stale cached content.
loadModel() schedules background kernel auto-tuning on first load without an explicit runtime/config opt-in, which introduces behavior-changing work outside the surfaced load contract.
Browser provider surface needs follow-up for fail-closed source selection and explicit auto-tuning policy.
Auditing decode readback cleanup for staging-buffer map failures and ring-slot advancement in generator steps.

Extracted try/finally readback helpers so single-token sampling and debug hidden-state staging buffers always unmap/destroy correctly, and decode-ring slots now still advance when sampled-token staging readback fails after submit.

generateNTokensGPU() still maps multiple staging buffers and destroys owned temporaries only on the all-success tail, so batch decode readback failures can still leave mapped buffers, owned staging/finiteness buffers, and the decode-ring slot in an inconsistent state.

Verified single-token and debug readback cleanup with tests/inference/generator-steps-cleanup.test.js plus focused runtime cleanup regressions; batch decode cleanup in this file still needs follow-up.
Generator-steps audit advanced: single-token and debug readback cleanup closed, batch decode readback cleanup remains open.
Closed the batch decode staging-buffer cleanup gap so mapped buffers, owned temporaries, and decode-ring advancement are deterministic on readback failure.
Generator-steps audit complete; single-token, debug, and batch decode readback cleanup invariants are now covered by focused runtime tests.
Added shared readBufferWithCleanup() so logits callers run temp-buffer cleanup on both successful and failed readback.
Logits readback utility audit complete; cleanup callback now closes readback ownership deterministically.
Moved final logits readback behind shared cleanup so temporary norm/matmul/logits buffers release even when readBuffer throws.
Logits pipeline audit complete; final readback no longer strands temporary runtime buffers on failure.
Wrapped chunked logits readback in shared cleanup so per-chunk buffers release correctly on readback failure.
Chunked logits GPU audit complete; per-chunk readback cleanup is deterministic.
Moved merged GPU logits readback behind shared cleanup so aggregation buffers release on error as well as success.
Multi-model network audit complete; merged logits readback cleanup verified.
Browser checkpoint save/load now closes IndexedDB handles after read/write completion and on setup failure.
Checkpoint runtime audit complete; browser checkpoint DB handles no longer leak across save/load cycles.
BPETokenizer.load() now resets vocab and merge state before reloading to avoid stale token accumulation.
BPE tokenizer audit complete; repeated load() calls now replace state instead of accumulating it.
BundledTokenizer.load() now resets vocab, merge, score, and byte-level state before reloading.
Bundled tokenizer audit complete; repeated load() calls now rebuild clean tokenizer state.
SentencePieceTokenizer now resets state before load() and again before byte-fallback init so partial parse state cannot bleed into fallback vocab.
SentencePiece tokenizer audit complete; reload and parse-fallback paths now rebuild deterministic state.
detectUnifiedMemory() now guards navigator/gpu access so Node runtimes fail closed instead of throwing during capability detection.
Unified-memory detection audit complete; non-browser runtimes now receive a deterministic unavailable result.
streamRange() now fails closed on incomplete fixed-length reads instead of yielding silent short streams.
clear() now invalidates queued and deduped loads by epoch so stale prefetch work cannot repopulate a new model cache.
Shard-cache audit complete; fixed-length stream reads fail closed and clear()/unload() no longer allow stale prefetch cache repopulation.
Streamed GPU shard assembly now throws on short upload and releases its temporary raw buffer on every failure path.
External setManifest()/unload() now keep the shared RDRR manifest singleton aligned with loader-owned manifest state.
DopplerLoader audit complete; streamed upload cleanup and manifest sync invariants are now explicit.
Expert cache eviction/clear now releases wrapped WeightBuffer entries, not only bare GPUBuffer instances.
Expert-cache audit complete; eviction and clear now free wrapped expert buffers deterministically.
Expert shard/tensor/size lookup now uses the loader context manifest directly instead of implicit global manifest state.
Expert-loader audit complete; expert metadata lookup no longer drifts with global manifest state.
RDRR expert-group helpers now accept an explicit manifest so runtime callers can avoid implicit singleton drift.
RDRR groups audit complete; expert-group lookup now supports explicit manifest ownership.
Tensor loader now releases owned upload/dequant/downcast buffers on throw paths and no longer assumes a global GPUBuffer constructor.
src/loader/tensors/tensor-loader.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Embedding loader now guards GPUBuffer checks so Float32Array and WeightBuffer paths stay valid in Node/test runtimes without a global GPUBuffer constructor.
src/loader/embedding-loader.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Final-weight loader now guards GPUBuffer checks so LM-head and tied-embedding paths fail closed without ReferenceError in Node/test runtimes.
src/loader/final-weights-loader.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Layer loader tensor selection now guards GPUBuffer checks so Float32Array/WeightBuffer paths remain valid in Node/test runtimes.
src/loader/layer-loader.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
LoaderState GPU-backed helpers now guard the global GPUBuffer constructor before type checks.
src/loader/loader-state.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Weight downcast now guards raw GPUBuffer detection so unsupported buffer types fail closed without ReferenceError.
src/loader/weight-downcast.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Checkpoint watch now accepts AbortSignal and exits cleanly instead of polling until stopWhenIdle or process exit.
src/training/checkpoint-watch.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Distillation checkpoint watch now threads AbortSignal through the shared watcher surface.
src/training/distillation/checkpoint-watch.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
LoRA watch now threads AbortSignal through the shared checkpoint watcher surface.
src/training/lora-pipeline.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
Training operator watch requests now forward an optional AbortSignal into distill and lora watch surfaces.
src/training/operator-command.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
MemoryMonitor and MemoryTimeSeries now clear an existing interval before restart so repeated start() calls do not orphan timers.
src/loader/memory-monitor.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
TrainingRunner now resets lastCheckpoint and lastArtifact at run start so reused runners do not expose stale run state.
src/training/runner.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
MoE router now destroys bias uniforms on every throw path, releases logits buffers in finally, and exposes explicit cached-router-buffer teardown.
src/inference/moe-router.js audit complete; regression coverage added and focused runtime checks passed.
